en: I know nothing compared to this young man.
ko: 나는 이 젊은이와 비교할 수 없어.

en: You ever hear of Gerald Lambeau? In 1905, there were hundreds of professors
ko: 제럴드 램보에 대해 들어본 적 있어?

en: renowned for their study of the universe,
ko: 1905년, 우주 연구로 유명한 교수들이 수백 명 있었어,

en: but it was a 26-year-old Swiss patent clerk
ko: 하지만 세상을 바꾼 것은 26세의 스위스 특허 사무원이었어.

en: doing physics in his spare time who changed the world.
Can you imagine if Einstein would have given that up just to get drunk with his buddies in Vienna every night?
ko: 만약 아인슈타인이 매일 밤 비엔나에서 친구들과 술 마시느라 포기했다면,

en: We all would have lost something. Tim would never have heard of him.
ko: 팀은 그를 절대 알지 못했을 거야.

en: Pretty dramatic, Jerry. No, it isn't, Sean.
ko: 아니, 그렇지 않아, 숀. 이 소년은 그 재능이 있어.

en: This boy has that gift. He just hasn't got the direction, but we can give that to him.
Hey, Jerry, in the 1960s, there was a young man graduated from University of Michigan,
did some brilliant work in mathematics, specifically bounded harmonic functions.
ko: 특히 제한된 조화 함수에 대해 연구했어.

en: Then he went on to Berkeley, was assistant professor, showed amazing potential.
Then he moved to Montana, and he blew the competition away.
ko: 테드 카진스키. 들어본 적 없어. 이봐, 팀! 요!

en: Yeah, so who was he? Ted Kaczynski.
Never heard of him. Hey, Timmy! Yo!
